content: WC3 competitive learning resources available: (1) Build orders: Ape Science WC3 Research Facility (community DB with steps, food counts, replays); wc3gymnasium (in-game overlay); Blizzard forums threads; (2) Replay analysis: wc3ai.com (APM, eAPM, hotkey patterns, hero builds from .w3g replay upload); wc3stats.com (replay-based stats); wc3v.com (compare-to-pro, letter-graded feedback on macro, production, item economy, idle resources, build order, tech, hero choices); (3) Community: WC3 Gym Discord (all levels); Back2Warcraft (Twitch casts, highest-level games); w3champions itself (ladder + stats); Liquipedia (tournament data); (4) Coaching: individual YouTube coaches (Ark for NE, others per race). GAPS in existing resources: No science-backed framework linking skills to learning theory; no structured progressive curriculum (everything is reference material, not learning paths); no tracking of YOUR progress against a curriculum; no single source for all races; coaching is expensive/inaccessible; no auto-detection of which concepts you have and haven't mastered.
